title: Research Source Catalogue Record (core, project-agnostic)
description: Authoritative metadata record for literature, standards, operational evidence, data exports, API schemas, images, transcripts, research artefacts and validation files, for any research project. The filename is a human-readable label only; this catalogue record is the source of truth. Fields marked '(project-controlled)' have their allowed values supplied by project_config.json, not hardcoded here. Run setup.py to produce schema.generated.json, which has those enums filled in for the active project.

catalogue_id.description: Stable identifier. Never reuse an identifier after assignment.

proposed_filename.description: Use the convention: CLASS_ARTEFACT_PRIMARY-ID_DATE_vNN_STATUS_ACCESS_SLUG_ORGANISATION_SYSTEM_ORIGIN.ext, single underscore between tokens, entirely upper-case except the extension. Standardized/controlled-vocabulary tokens lead (quick to scan and group in a flat folder); descriptive/contextual tokens (what the file is about, company, source directory) trail.

ingest_date.description: Date the file entered the research repository.

document_date.description: Use YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM, YYYY, or null. Do not guess.

source_version.description: Version stated by the source, e.g. 'DCSA Booking 2.0' or 'DMN 1.4'.

source_organisation.description: (project-controlled) Allowed values come from project_config.json -> organisations.

source_system.description: (project-controlled) Allowed values come from project_config.json -> systems.

artefact_type.description: (project-controlled) Allowed values come from vocabularies_core.json -> recommended_artefact_types plus project_config.json -> extended_artefact_types.

primary_entity_type.description: Generic entity kinds. Add project-specific entity kinds via project_config.json -> extended_entity_types if needed.

domain_identifiers.description: Free-form map of project-specific identifier arrays (e.g. container_numbers, booking_numbers, job_ids, event_ids, rule_ids). Keys are defined per project in project_config.json -> domain_identifier_fields.

research_taxonomy.description: Free-form map of project-specific classification tags (e.g. framework_components, lifecycle_stages, readiness_dimensions, rq_mapping for this MPhil project; a different project would define its own keys). Keys and allowed values are defined in project_config.json -> research_taxonomies.

verification_basis.description: Explain exactly what was verified and how. Do not use vague statements.

content_preview.description: Short extracted or OCR'd preview of file content, capped at project_config.json -> preview_max_words words.

summary.description: Free-text, one-sentence human-readable context: document purpose (e.g. quote, gate pass), the system/platform that likely produced it, and any client/counterparty/business it concerns. Not constrained to project_config.json's organisation/system vocab and never used in proposed_filename - this is for a reader (e.g. in a research paper) to understand a record at a glance without decoding the filename.

source_group_id.description: Shared identifier (slug of the base filename) linking files that are repeat exports/downloads of the same underlying report or document, e.g. 'job-management (11).csv' and 'job-management (20).csv'. Independent of duplicate_group_id/canonical_catalogue_id: members can have different content (later exports) and are never auto-excluded because of this grouping alone.

project_id.description: Slug of the owning research project, from project_config.json -> project_id.
